title: Licence + provenance build gate (fails CI on unverified/incompatible source or dangling citation)
context: The source-register schema (task 001) defines how sources are recorded; the repo skeleton (task 003) provides the CI pipeline. This task implements the enforcement layer: a build gate that fails CI whenever content cites an unverified source, uses a source beyond its allowed-use rights, or has a dangling citation with no matching sources.yaml entry. This is non-negotiable: no content may ship citing an unverified or incompatible source.
objective: Implement the licence + provenance build gate as a CI step that enforces the source-register schema: dangling citations, incompatible allowed-use, and missing licence-verification dates all fail the build.
acceptanceCriteria: CI fails if any content citation references a source ID with no matching entry in data/sources.yaml (dangling reference check).
CI fails if content uses a source with allowedUse 'link' or 'quote' in a 'reproduce' or 'adapt' context (licence-compatibility check).
CI fails if any sources.yaml entry is missing licenceVerifiedDate or verifiedBy.
Link-checking step runs as part of CI and fails on broken URLs in sources.yaml or content citations.
The gate is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md: how to add a source, what allowedUse values mean, and how to fix each failure mode.
At least one passing test case (a valid citation) and one failing test case per failure mode are included in the test suite.
Maintainer + Licence steward review sign-off recorded.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Solution approach & architecture — Build/validation tooling; § Data, licensing & compliance — Provenance model)
output: A merged PR adding the licence + provenance build gate to the CI pipeline, with tests covering all failure modes and CONTRIBUTING documentation.
